body: Routing to the team for assistance: @JonathanCrd

## đźŽŻ Agentic Issue Triage

Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member best able to assist.

**Summary:** `ManagedIdentityCredential` (Azure.Identity 1.13.1) intermittently fails with a `SocketException (10013)` â€” "access permissions denied" â€” when connecting to the IMDS endpoint `169.254.169.254:80` on Azure App Service. The issue is intermittent, production-specific, and exhausts all 6 retry attempts.

<details>
<summary>đź”Ť Issue Analysis</summary>

**Root cause hypothesis:**  
`SocketException 10013` (`WSAEACCES`) typically means the OS/network stack rejected the connection at a socket level â€” not an IMDS rate limit. On Azure App Service, this can occur when:
- A firewall or NSG rule intermittently blocks outbound traffic to the link-local range `169.254.x.x`
- The App Service sandbox restricts raw socket access during high CPU/memory pressure
- A platform-side networking event (SNAT exhaustion, sandbox resource limits) temporarily blocks the connection

This differs from an IMDS rate-limit (HTTP 429), which would surface as an HTTP error rather than a socket-level rejection.

**Why production but not dev?**  
Production environments tend to have stricter firewall/NSG configurations, higher load (more SNAT connections), or tighter App Service plan resource limits.

</details>

<details>
<summary>đź› ď¸Ź Debugging Strategies</summary>

1. **Check App Service networking diagnostics:**  
   In the Azure Portal â†’ App Service â†’ Diagnose and solve problems â†’ "Network checks" to look for SNAT exhaustion or connectivity issues to `169.254.169.254`.

2. **Validate IMDS reachability:**  
   Use the App Service Kudu console (Advanced Tools) to run:  
   ```
   curl -H "Metadata: true" "http://169.254.169.254/metadata/identity/oauth2/token?api-version=2018-02-01&resource=https://vault.azure.net/"
   ```

3. **Enable verbose Azure.Identity logging** to capture token request timelines:
   ```csharp
   AzureEventSourceListener.CreateConsoleLogger(EventLevel.Verbose);
   ```

4. **Reuse `ManagedIdentityCredential`:**  
   Create the credential once (e.g., as a singleton) rather than creating a new instance per Hangfire job invocation â€” this avoids repeated cold-start IMDS calls.

5. **Consider `DefaultAzureCredential` with `ManagedIdentityClientId`** as a fallback pattern.

</details>

<details>
<summary>đźŹ·ď¸Ź Label Selection</summary>

- **Category:** `Client` â€” This is a bug/behavior issue in the Azure.Identity client SDK (`Azure.Identity` package)
- **Service:** `Azure.Identity` â€” Explicitly stated in the issue (version 1.13.1); confirmed by multiple reference issues with the same label pair (e.g., #56525, #52122, #50268)
- **Confidence:** High â€” the package name is explicitly stated, the category is unambiguous (client SDK, not management or provisioning)

</details>

<details>
<summary>đź”— Relevant Resources</summary>

- [Azure.Identity Managed Identity Credential docs](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/azure.identity.managedidentitycredential)
- [Troubleshoot managed identity on App Service](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/app-service/overview-managed-identity)
- [IMDS endpoint documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/virtual-machines/instance-metadata-service)
- [Azure SDK retry documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/azure/sdk/azure-sdk-for-dotnet-threadpool-and-async)

</details>
